danger_signals.meaning: Active aerial threat; destroying complete formations triggers power-ups

danger_signals.visual_form: Large naval vessel sprites at stage end
danger_signals.meaning: Major escalation in threat level; required defeat to progress
Time-pressure danger; constant threat of mission failure

danger_signals.visual_form: Projectile spray from aircraft and ships

danger_signals.meaning: All-aerial battle stages; higher sustained threat vs. ship stages
Final boss; ultimate mission objective and maximum danger

safety_signals.meaning: Reward state; player performance advantage triggered
Major fuel restoration (24 units); increased survivability
Complete energy bar refill; maximum safety state

safety_signals.visual_form: Cycling power-up sprites (Shotgun, Three-way MG, Auto, Shell, Laser)
safety_signals.meaning: Player firepower increased; tactical advantage vs. threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Two wingmen sprites flanking player aircraft
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced damage output and visual ally presence

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pacific theater; Midway Atoll vicinity; player's single-plane perspective

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Red plane formations; Japanese naval vessels (battleships, carriers)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Strategic bombers, heavy bombers, naval gun emplacements
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Yamato battleship (flagship); sea-based naval formations; air squadrons

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mixed aircraft and naval vessel enemies; lighter enemy density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard enemy patrol patterns; boss battleship/carrier at stage end

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen filled with strategic bombers or single heavy bomber sprite
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sustained aerial combat; heavy bomber requires engine destruction for defeat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Energy bar visual warning (color change or flashing)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player cannot use special attacks; mission failure imminent without power-up collection

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Largest battleship sprite; maximum screen presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ultimate boss mechanics; player's final objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Power-up drops on screen; weapon upgrade cycles available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Temporary tactical superiority; window to upgrade weapons

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Two P-38 sprites on screen simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Energy transfer mechanic when aircraft overlap; shared fuel pool

environmental_storytelling.example: 11 stages feature naval vessel bosses; visual progression from smaller ships to Yamato communicates escalating threat and historical Pacific campaign
5 all-aerial stages use different bomber types (strategic vs. heavy); communicates enemy force diversity and changing engagement context

environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: Constant depletion creates war mission urgency; supplies are finite resource
environmental_storytelling.example: Player must manage fuel consumption like real combat pilot; every maneuver has resource cost
Midway Atoll location, sea-based combat, Japanese naval assets (Yamato) establish WW2 Pacific naval context without explicit exposition

environmental_storytelling.technique: Red vs. player aircraft color coding
environmental_storytelling.example: Enemy formations shown in red; visual opposition to player's (implied) opposing color; instant faction recognition

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Organized squadron movement; destruction of complete formation triggers power-up reward
Sustained gun fire; requires multiple hits to defeat; controls stage scrolling pause via Tsunami attack
Requires engine-specific destruction; more complex defeat condition than smaller aircraft
Can be shot to cycle between weapon types and energy restoration; communicates player agency in upgrade selection
Cannot use special attacks; forced evasion-only tactics; visual warning of mission failure proximity
Flank player aircraft; enhance damage output; can be destroyed by enemy fire; temporary tactical asset
Largest sprite on screen; final objective; communicates mission culmination through visual dominance

notes: 1943 uses minimalist NES-era visuals to communicate world-state through sprite size/color hierarchy, formation patterns, and stage structure. The game's core danger/safety loop is fuel management: danger is constant (depleting meter), safety requires enemy destruction (power-ups) or strategic bombing (Yamato as final fuel sink). Faction identity is communicated entirely through color (red = enemy) and vehicle type (P-38 vs. Japanese naval/air assets). Regional differences (JP: 'Battle of Valhalla' vs. US: 'Battle of Midway') suggest ideological framing but don't affect visual communication of game state. The 16-stage progression (11 sea + 5 air) creates alternating engagement contexts that visually reinforce campaign progression.
